# toStandardSchema()

> **Server Factory Layer**

```ts
function toStandardSchema<A, I>(schema: Schema<A, I>): {
  ~standard: {
     version: 1;
     vendor: string;
     validate: (value: unknown) => 
        | {
        value: A;
        issues?: undefined;
      }
        | {
        value?: undefined;
        issues: {
           message: string;
           path: {
              key: PropertyKey;
           }[];
        }[];
     };
     jsonSchema: {
        input: () => Record<string, unknown>;
        output: () => Record<string, unknown>;
     };
  };
};
```

Defined in: [src/app/server/standard-schema-bridge.ts:67](https://github.com/EmperorRAG/first-mcp/blob/3d2831b148eaa4c84c57fa062fce6df920f72a50/src/app/server/standard-schema-bridge.ts#L67)

Adapts an Effect [Schema.Schema](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) into the `StandardSchemaWithJSON`
interface required by the MCP SDK's `registerTool()`.

## Type Parameters

| Type Parameter | Description                              |
| -------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `A`            | The decoded (output) type of the schema. |
| `I`            | The encoded (input) type of the schema.  |

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type                                                      | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| --------- | --------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `schema`  | [`Schema`](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/)<`A`, `I`> | The Effect [Schema.Schema](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) to adapt. Must be JSON-Schema-compatible (no opaque transformations that [JSONSchema.make](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) cannot represent). |

## Returns

```ts
{
  ~standard: {
     version: 1;
     vendor: string;
     validate: (value: unknown) => 
        | {
        value: A;
        issues?: undefined;
      }
        | {
        value?: undefined;
        issues: {
           message: string;
           path: {
              key: PropertyKey;
           }[];
        }[];
     };
     jsonSchema: {
        input: () => Record<string, unknown>;
        output: () => Record<string, unknown>;
     };
  };
}
```

A `StandardSchemaWithJSON`-compatible object that can be passed
directly to the MCP SDK's `registerTool()` `inputSchema` option.

## Remarks

The returned object carries a `~standard` property (version `1`,
vendor `"effect"`) with two capabilities:

- **`validate(value)`** — Runs [Schema.decodeUnknownEither](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) against
  the provided `value`.  On success (`Right`) it returns
  `{ value: decodedValue }`.  On failure (`Left`) it formats the
  [ParseResult](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) errors via
  [ParseResult.ArrayFormatter.formatErrorSync](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) and returns an
  `{ issues }` array where each issue contains a `message` and a `path`
  of `{ key }` segments.

- **`jsonSchema`** — Exposes `input()` and `output()` accessors that both
  return the same JSON Schema record produced by [JSONSchema.make](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/).
  The record is computed once at construction time and spread into a
  plain `Record<string, unknown>` to satisfy the SDK's type expectation.

## Example

```ts
import { Schema } from "effect";
import { toStandardSchema } from "./standard-schema-bridge.js";

const InputSchema = Schema.Struct({ id: Schema.String });
server.registerTool("my-tool", {
  inputSchema: toStandardSchema(InputSchema),
}, handler);
```
